item.title: Version 0.13.2
item.description:
v0.13.2 — Selection drag actually works (rearchitected)

The previous attempt at fixing drag-to-select (v0.13.0, which switched .onTapGesture to .simultaneousGesture) didn't go far enough — selection was still broken because TWO SwiftUI modifiers were eating the mouseDown stream that SwiftTerm needs. This release rips both out and replaces them with pure AppKit equivalents.

  • SwiftUI gestures and contextMenu removed from the pane cell. Both .simultaneousGesture(TapGesture()...) AND .contextMenu { ... } install NSGestureRecognizers on the NSViewRepresentable hosting view. Combined, they were swallowing the leading edge of every mouseDragged so SwiftTerm's drag-to-select never started. Click-and-drag in the terminal area now goes straight to SwiftTerm with zero SwiftUI in the way.
  • Pane focus tracking moved to a window-scoped NSEvent monitor. A new NSEvent.addLocalMonitorForEvents(.leftMouseDown) installed alongside the existing key monitor (lifecycle tied to hostedWindow) walks the responder chain on each click to find the underlying TermyTerminalView and update tab.activePaneId + sessions.selectedTabId accordingly. The monitor only READS the event (returns it unchanged), so SwiftTerm gets the full event stream.
  • Right-click context menu is now a native AppKit NSMenu. Installed via view.menu = TermyTerminalView.makeContextMenu() in makeNSView. Copy / Paste / Select All target the responder chain (so SwiftTerm's own validate-menu-item logic auto-disables Copy when there's no selection, Paste when the clipboard is empty, etc.). Find / Clear / New Tab / Split Horizontally / Split Vertically post their existing notifications via a tiny singleton NotificationDispatcher.
  • Active-pane border overlay marked non-hit-testable. SwiftUI shapes are hit-testable along the stroke path by default, so the 1pt accent ring around the active pane was eating clicks that landed exactly on the border. .allowsHitTesting(false) on the overlay makes it purely decorative.

Net effect: drag-to-select, double-click word selection, triple-click line selection, shift-click extend, copy/paste/select-all from the right-click menu — all work on the first click, every time, in any pane (single or split).

item.title: Version 0.13.1
item.description:
v0.13.1 — Previews use the user's font + theme everywhere

Pass-2 polish on the Settings sheet — every visual preview now renders with the same font family and theme palette the live pane uses, so picking a font or a theme shows the same glyphs and colors in the preview as you'll see seconds later in the terminal.

  • Theme preview cards use your font. Sample text inside each theme card now renders with settings.fontFamily (SF Mono / Menlo / JetBrains Mono / Hack / whatever you picked under Settings → Font) instead of generic .system(.monospaced). So changing font family is visible across the theme picker too — every card switches together. Cursor in each card still matches the cursor style you chose (block / bar / underline) in each theme's cursor color.
  • Cursor-style chips show the active theme's cursor color. The block/bar/underline chips used to render the cursor in the system accent (blue everywhere) regardless of theme. Now they use theme.cursorColor + theme.foregroundColor for the demo glyph and the theme's accent color for the selection ring — pick a chip under Sunset and see the amber cursor you'll actually get.
  • Density preview uses theme colors. Compact / Cozy / Spacious cards used hardcoded green/white/blue. Now they read theme.ansi[2] (prompt), theme.ansi[4] (directory), theme.foreground (text), theme.cursorColor (cursor) and theme.accentColor (selection ring), in the user's font family. Picking a padding preset is no longer a context-switch out of your theme.
  • Opacity preview uses your theme foreground + font. The sample "Sample terminal text" inside the opacity swatch now renders in the actual theme foreground at the user's font, so what you see at 70% opacity is what you'll see on the pane.

item.title: Version 0.13.0
item.description:
v0.13.0 — Honest theme previews, polished session logs, smooth selection

An audit pass through Settings — every toggle/slider/picker now actually does what its UI claims — plus log polish and a selection-drag bug fix.

  • Theme previews now show what the theme actually changes. The old preview painted a fake mini "tab strip" with the theme accent as a bottom-stroke — Termy's real tab bar does no such thing (active tabs use neutral primary tint, not the theme accent), so the preview was misleading on every theme. The card now renders only surfaces the theme genuinely drives: foreground text in the theme color, a "selected" word in the theme's selection color, a cursor in the theme's cursor color rendered in the SHAPE the user picked under Settings → Cursor (block / bar / underline), a thin accent border matching the active-pane stroke PaneLayout draws, and the full 16-color ANSI strip. Pick a theme and what you see in the picker is what you get in the pane.
  • Settings audit: every toggle is wired. Walked each @Published var in TerminalSettings through to its apply site (font/cursor/scrollback to SwiftTerm, recording to SessionRecorder, idle threshold to the activity heuristic, etc.). One dead theme field — urlColor/urlAccent, which had no consumer because SwiftTerm doesn't expose a hyperlink color hook — was removed from the model and all 17 theme definitions, so nothing in the codebase pretends to drive a knob that doesn't exist.
  • Session logs are now plain text (.txt) with a clean format. Extension switched from .log to .txt so editors and Quick Look don't apply log-syntax highlighting that mis-tints the output. Replaced the unicode box-drawing header (which warped in many editors because the right edge wasn't even closed) with flush-left ASCII: Termy session log + === rule + identity lines. Scene breaks are now [HH:mm:ss] cwd flush-left — no triangle marker, no 2-space body indent. Adjacent blank lines collapse to one. Trailing whitespace on each line is trimmed. Old .log files still appear in the browser so you don't lose history.
  • "Delete all logs" button in Settings → Advanced → Session recording. Shows the file count + total size live; disabled when there's nothing to delete; confirm dialog before the wipe. Active recordings keep running into fresh files via POSIX unlink semantics — no coordination needed with live panes.
  • Selection drag works again. SwiftUI's .onTapGesture on each pane was running its tap-vs-drag disambiguation state machine, holding the mouseDown long enough to eat the leading edge of SwiftTerm's drag-to-select. Replaced with .simultaneousGesture(TapGesture()...) so click-to-focus still fires but the underlying NSView mouse stream is uninterrupted. Drag-to-select, double-click word, triple-click line — all responsive on the first try now.

item.title: Version 0.12.0
item.description:
v0.12.0 — Smooth scroll lock, visible inactive caret, drag-drop from Finder, dev bundle

A focused round on stability + polish, plus a perf pass and a dedicated staging app for iteration.

  • Scroll while output is streaming is no longer glitchy. v0.11.2's lock worked but felt jittery — the lock target was updated async from the scroll-wheel monitor, so chunks arriving between a scroll and the async update would pull the viewport back to the wrong row. The lock state is now recomputed synchronously per data chunk: lockedRow updates to current yDisp if the user is scrolled away, releases if they're back at the tail. The sync restore lands in the same render frame as the snap-to-bottom, so the bounce never reaches drawRect. Spurious lock-at-launch (scrollPosition returns 0 on fresh panes / after clear / alt-buffer) is also gated out — autoscroll now resumes the moment you scroll back to the bottom.
  • Cursor visible in every split pane. SwiftTerm draws a 3pt hollow stroke caret when its view isn't first responder, but on bright wallpapers the stroke blends with the foreground theme color and is effectively invisible. Inactive panes now also paint a brighter filled overlay (theme foreground, 55% fill + 95% border, 1.5pt) at the SwiftTerm caret's exact frame, so you can always see where the cursor lives in inactive splits. Active pane is unchanged (SwiftTerm's filled block). Overlay tracks the cursor as the shell moves it. Single-pane caret-at-launch is also fixed — a forced appearance refresh after the focus claim catches the case where SwiftTerm positioned the caret while the view's frame was still .zero.
  • Drop files from Finder into the terminal. Drag a file (or multi-select several) from Finder onto any pane — the shell-quoted path(s) get typed at the cursor, space-separated. Paths with spaces, single quotes, backticks, or shell-substitution characters are POSIX-safely single-quoted. Dropping on an inactive pane focuses it and inserts there. The pane shows a 2pt accent border on drag-enter so the target is obvious.
  • Pane close X no longer sits behind the scrollbar. SwiftTerm pins its NSScroller to the top edge via Auto Layout, which put the per-pane "close X" button literally on top of the scroll-up arrow. The scrollbar now starts 26pt below the pane's top (Auto Layout constraint replaced — overriding the frame in layout() just gets clobbered on the next pass), giving the X a clear, unambiguous hit area. The X's original discrete styling is preserved.
  • Termy Dev: dedicated staging app. ./stage.sh builds a debug binary and installs it to /Applications/Termy Dev.app (bundle id com.mees.termy.dev) — separate process, separate UserDefaults domain, photo-negative dock icon so you can't confuse it with prod. Used as the iteration target for in-progress changes; prod stays untouched while you're working in a Termy session. ./release.sh still cuts the prod release to /Applications/Termy.app.
  • Perf pass. ANSI CSI regex cached as a static (was re-compiled per output line in the trigger scan + per recentVisibleText() call). Per-pane idle timer 0.5s → 1.0s (halves runloop wakes with N active panes). Cwd persist debounce 0.5s → 1.0s (less UserDefaults churn on cd-heavy workflows). Git branch poll 4s → 10s (still debounced through .git/HEAD). StatusBar clock now only writes @State on actual minute rollover, not every 30s tick — halves StatusBar re-renders.

item.title: Version 0.11.2
item.description:
v0.11.2 — Scroll lock + less sensitive tab rename

Two focused fixes on top of v0.11.1's scrollback work.

  • The viewport stops yanking back to the bottom while you're trying to read. Scrolling up — by mouse wheel, trackpad, PageUp, ⌘⇧↑, or scrollbar drag — now locks the viewport at whatever row you landed on. Subsequent output from claude or whatever's running keeps accumulating in the buffer below, but YOUR view stays put. Use ⌘⇧↓ (or scroll/PageDown back to the live tail) to release the lock; the next chunk of output auto-snaps to the bottom again, same as it always did before you scrolled. SwiftTerm's built-in userScrolling flag only triggers on scrollbar drag — wheel/trackpad scrolls were leaving the lock disengaged and the viewport snapping back on every PTY chunk, which made reading claude's earlier paragraph mid-stream impossible. Termy now tracks scroll-away state itself and restores yDisp async after SwiftTerm's internal ensureCaretIsVisible snap.
  • Tab title rename no longer fires on accidental click. The double-tap-to-rename gesture is gone; rename is now exclusively right-click → "Rename Tab…". The combined single+double tap gesture was adding a ~250 ms delay to every tab switch (SwiftUI's gesture system has to wait for the double-tap window before firing the single-tap), and even with that delay users were tripping into rename mode whenever they double-clicked too quickly to switch tabs. Switching tabs is now instant and predictable.

item.title: Version 0.11.1
item.description:
v0.11.1 — Scrollback stays put + smarter notifications + perf cache

Follow-up to v0.11.0. The aggressive "wipe scrollback on every resize / every TUI clear-screen" of v0.11.0 fixed cascading welcome banners but nuked legitimate history in the process — closing a split pane was deleting hours of conversation in the surviving pane. v0.11.1 keeps scrollback intact while still suppressing the TUI cascade, plus cleans up the notification spam and shaves real CPU off the hot path.

  • Scrollback no longer gets deleted on split close or window resize. The previous unconditional \e[3J on any >30% size change is gone. Verified: 100 lines of shell output stay intact through split → close → resize → claude session → more resizes.
  • TUI cascade is now suppressed adaptively. Termy tracks how often full-screen clears (\e[2J) happen — a single one (the clear command) leaves scrollback alone, but ≥3 in a 5-second window flips the pane into "TUI mode" and auto-trims the now-stale renders for the next 30s. Net effect: claude's welcome banner stops stacking 4× after splits, AND your shell scrollback survives the same operation.
  • Notifications redesigned. Title now uses the cwd basename ("termy" instead of generic "Termy") so you can tell at a glance WHICH project pinged you. ✓/🔔/⚠ glyphs on subtitles. Dedupe inside a 30-second window so the same body can't fire 6× in a row. The noisy idle-heuristic path is now suppressed by default — only OSC 133 shell-integration "command finished" pings fire (re-enable with defaults write com.mees.termy termy.notifyOnlyOSC133 -bool false).
  • Performance pass on the hot path. dataReceived now caches every per-byte UserDefaults read (cinemaMode, cinemaCps, recordSessions, idleThreshold) via a single CFPreferences-change observer instead of re-reading 4× per PTY chunk. The OSC 133 scanner now early-exits when the chunk contains no ESC byte, which saves a per-byte loop on the common case of plain command output. Idle-detection timer now tears down when the pane settles instead of ticking forever at 2 Hz per pane.
  • SwiftTerm draw fix from v0.11.0 retained: split-close no longer leaves a blank pane (was caused by scrollTo(Int.max/2) poisoning yDisp); focus actually moves to the new pane after ⌘T / ⌘D; Buffer.isCursorInViewPort overflow crash sidestepped; scrollback default still 10000 lines.

item.title: Version 0.11.0
item.description:
v0.11.0 — Multi-pane reliability + scrollback fixes

Heavy stability pass on tabs, splits, and resizing — every reported case of "history disappears", "new tab clones the active one", "split wipes content", "blank pane after closing a split", and "claude welcome banner stacking 4× in scrollback" is fixed.

  • New tab is independent. Previously a new tab silently shared the previous tab's NSView because SwiftUI couldn't tell two single-pane PaneLayouts apart — now all tabs are mounted at all times in a ZStack with the inactive ones parked off-screen, so switching tabs preserves every pane's scrollback with zero re-parenting cost.
  • Split-close no longer leaves a blank pane. Root cause was Termy calling scrollTo(row: Int.max/2), which SwiftTerm doesn't clamp — that poisoned yDisp and made every visible line draw off-screen. Removed.
  • Focus actually moves after split / ⌘T. The previous focus handler gated on shouldClaimFocus, which refuses to displace another TerminalView — so keystrokes kept landing in the OLD pane after every split. Tab/pane switch is now authoritative and takes focus unconditionally; the .onChange that should have fired on activePaneId never did (SwiftUI doesn't observe sub-object @Published changes through a parent), so a notification was added.
  • Scrollback bumped 500 → 10 000 lines. SwiftTerm's 500-line default was getting blown through immediately whenever a split halved the pane width — every wide line wrapped 2-3× and the oldest history fell off the bottom of the buffer. 10 000 covers hours of work.
  • TUI scrollback pollution on resize is fixed. Apps that don't use alt-screen (claude in particular) redraw their entire UI on every SIGWINCH — that previously left stacked copies of the welcome banner cascading down scrollback after every split / drag-resize. Termy now sends CSI 3 J (erase scrollback) when the pane changes by more than 30 % in either dimension, both on programmatic resizes AND on user drag-resize end. Setting termy.clearScrollbackOnSignificantResize in defaults disables it if you want full history preservation.
  • Cinema mode actually paces output now. The cps slider was being clamped to 30 minimum and then defeated by a per-tick "auto catch-up" that divided the queue by 8 — a 200-character paragraph rendered in under a second regardless of setting. Drain logic rewritten to honour cps; tiny echo bursts of typed characters bypass the pacer so typing doesn't feel laggy.
  • Crash fixed: arithmetic overflow in Buffer.isCursorInViewPort. SwiftTerm's formula (yBase + 2·yDisp) overflowed with deep scrollback; we stopped calling it.
  • Idle timer tears down when the pane settles instead of ticking forever — saves a wakeup per pane per second indefinitely.
  • Install instructions clarified for the Gatekeeper "Apple could not verify" warning that hits every first-time downloader — the one-line xattr -dr com.apple.quarantine bypass is now front-and-centre in the README.

item.title: Version 0.10.1
item.description: Polish pass: cheatsheet now lists every shortcut (Agent Sessions / Session Logs / Paste History / Quick Select / Use Selection for Find / jump-to-prompt / scroll-to-edge — was missing from the in-app help), Diagnostics report actually shows the env Termy advertises to tools (TERM_PROGRAM, LC_TERMINAL, TERM_FEATURES — were showing as "—"), Command Palette can no longer crash when switching filter category, modal overlays shrink to fit small windows, `termy://palette` / `termy://welcome` / `termy://diagnostics` now reliably show on URL invocation.

item.title: Version 0.10.0 — Stability + transcript-quality logs
item.description:
Fixed

  • Window resize no longer wipes scrollback. The reflow-disabled wrapper was permanently trimming history every time the cell grid changed (font bumps, splits, drag-resize). Let SwiftTerm reflow natively and your full session history survives.
  • Caret fills + accepts input on launch. Cold-launch / tab-restore previously left the terminal view unfocused — you had to click once before typing. The pane now claims first responder on window attach + windowDidBecomeKey, and tab switches carry focus to the new pane.
  • Split-view close button cleared from the scrollbar. The per-pane × was overlapping SwiftTerm's scroller when a tab had multiple panes.
  • Recording indicator no longer looks like a camera. Swapped the red record.circle for a subtle tray.and.arrow.down in tertiary tone.

New

  • Activity stripe. A thin animated bar pulses at the top of any pane that's currently producing output — claude/codex thinking, builds compiling, watchers ticking. Driven by the same idle heuristic that powers "command finished" notifications, so it works for anything without shell integration. TimelineView-based, can't desync on rapid toggles. Toggle in Settings.
  • Hover-to-discover links. URLs and OSC 8 hyperlinks now underline on plain hover and open on a single click — no Cmd modifier required. Works equally in plain shell and inside Claude / Codex.
  • Clear all session logs. Destructive button in the Session Logs browser header, confirmation-gated. Wipes ~/Library/Application Support/Termy/sessions/ in one click.

Polish

  • Session logs read like a transcript. Full rewrite of the recorder: a cell-cursor model collapses zsh's CR + autosuggestion redraws to their final visible state. Alternate-screen runs (claude / vim / less) get summarised as [interactive program — Xm Ys] instead of polluting the log with redraw noise. Header carries date / user / shell / cwd / Termy version.
  • Notifications follow Apple HIG. Title is always "Termy", subtitle is the event ("Command finished" / "Bell" / your trigger title), body is in ~/dir · 10:13 — or the actual command output when it's meaningful. Prompt-shaped previews (the noisy user@host~% redraw leak in the old build) are filtered out.

item.title: Version 0.9.33 — Bucket A complete
item.description:
QuickSelect (⌘⇧/) — final Bucket A item

  • ⌘⇧/ opens Quick Select. Scans the active pane's recent output for URLs, file paths, git hashes, and IPs. Picker shows them with kind labels; ↵ copies, ⌘↵ opens (URL via browser, file via your configured editor). Filter field for narrowing.
  • Termy's variant trades WezTerm's overlay-on-the-terminal for a focused modal — easier to scan when you have 20+ matches and the picker can dedup repeated paths (a path appearing 10 times in scrollback shows once).

Bucket A from the competitor research roadmap is now complete (8/8). All shipped across v0.9.27 → v0.9.33 in this session: OSC 8 → editor, Diagnostics, YAML workflows, Trigger packs, Jump-to-prompt, QuickSelect, Paste history, Agent Panel.

item.title: Version 0.9.32
item.description:
Agent Panel — Bucket A #8 (Termy's take on Warp's agent manager)

  • ⌘⇧A opens an Agent Sessions panel that reads Claude Code's local JSONL transcripts under ~/.claude/projects/. Sidebar lists every recent session across every project (up to 60), filterable. Detail pane shows last user message, last assistant reply (text content extracted from the message blocks), the decoded project path, and the session id.
  • "Open new tab in this directory" button — click to spawn a fresh Termy tab cd'd into the session's project root. Quickest path from "where did I leave that Claude conversation?" to working there again.
  • Why this is better than Warp's Agent Management Panel: Warp just lists active tabs, while Termy reads files Claude Code already writes (no first-party API needed) so you get full per-session context across past + present sessions, not just current ones.

item.title: Version 0.9.31
item.description:
Diagnostics view (A #2 from the roadmap)

  • Settings → About → Run Diagnostics… opens a sheet showing exactly what Termy is currently advertising to tools that probe terminal capabilities: TERM / TERM_PROGRAM / LC_TERMINAL / TERM_FEATURES env vars, graphics-protocol parsers active, OSC 133 / OSC 8 setup state, notification settings, recording state. Plus a "Copy report to clipboard" button so the user can paste it directly into a GitHub issue when something isn't working.
  • Cuts down "why isn't imgcat rendering?" / "why isn't my OSC 133 setup firing?" support load by making detection state visible.

item.title: Version 0.9.30
item.description:
Trigger packs — Bucket A #4 (Termy's take on iTerm2 triggers)

Termy's design choice vs iTerm2's free-form trigger rows: toggleable packs, like ad-blocker filter lists. Common cases are one-tap. Settings → General → Triggers.

  • Claude Code pack: notify on "do you want me to proceed", "may i", "compacting context", "all done" etc. The "needs approval" trigger plays sound regardless of global sound setting.
  • OpenAI Codex pack: notify on Codex approval prompts ("approve", "y/n", "press enter to continue").
  • Build / test errors pack: notify on "error:", "fatal", "panic", "failed", "exception", "tests failed", "assertion failed".

Each pane runs the active trigger set against every newline-terminated line of output (with ANSI stripped); first match per line fires once. All three packs default to OFF — opt in via Settings.

item.title: Version 0.9.29
item.description:
Jump-to-prompt navigation (⌘↑ / ⌘↓) — Bucket A #5

  • ⌘↑ / ⌘↓ jumps to previous / next prompt in scrollback. Set up the OSC 133 shell snippet (README → Smart command-finished notifications) and Termy tracks every prompt; the shortcuts then scroll directly between commands instead of fiddling with the trackpad through 200 lines of npm install output.
  • Falls back to half-page scroll when no OSC 133 marks are recorded (shells without the snippet), so the shortcut is always useful.
  • Each pane keeps its own ring buffer of up to 1000 prompt marks; oldest dropped on overflow.

item.title: Version 0.9.28
item.description:
Paste history (⌘⇧V) — Bucket A #7 from the roadmap

  • ⌘⇧V opens a paste-history picker. Last 20 strings copied to your pasteboard, recent on top, filter field at top. Pick → typed into the active pane. Standard pattern lifted from 1Password / Paste / Raycast, but scoped to Termy and never persisted to disk (in-memory only, vibecoders pasting API keys benefit).
  • Polled changeCount-driven capture (500ms tick) so it picks up copies from anywhere on the system, not just inside Termy.
  • "Clear" button in the picker; right-click any row to re-copy or paste directly.
  • Same chrome (640×480, .regularMaterial, accent header) as Settings / Command Palette / Cheatsheet / Session Logs — all five primary modals are visually identical.

item.title: Version 0.9.27
item.description:
Acting on the competitor research: OSC 8 → editor + YAML workflows

From the Bucket A roadmap (Warp / iTerm2 / WezTerm / Ghostty / Wave research). First two ship here:

  • OSC 8 hyperlinks open in your editor. When a tool emits a clickable file path (Claude/Codex output, rg --hyperlink-format=kitty, eza, delta, bat) or even a plain path:line:col, Termy now routes the click to your preferred editor (Cursor / VSCode / Zed / Sublime / TextMate) at the right line. Picker in Settings → Appearance → "Editor for clickable file paths".
  • YAML workflows. Drop ~/.termy/workflows/*.yaml snippets with {{argument}} placeholders; they show up in the Command Palette under a new Workflows filter. Project-local .termy/workflows/ overrides global. Minimal parser handles the Warp YAML shape. Default values fire on selection (interactive arg-fill is the next iteration). Example: 
    name: Resume Claude
command: claude --resume
description: Reattach to the most recent session

item.title: Version 0.9.26
item.description:
Dynamic window titles

  • NSWindow.title now reflects the active pane — Window menu, Dock right-click "windows" list, and Mission Control labels can finally tell multi-window users which Termy window is which. Title source: custom tab title → pane title (if non-default) → cwd basename with ~ folding.
  • The visible chrome stays unchanged (title bar still hidden) — this only feeds the macOS chrome around the window.

item.title: Version 0.9.25
item.description:
⌘E Use Selection for Find + Help menu links

  • ⌘E "Use Selection for Find" — select text in any pane, press ⌘E → find bar opens prefilled with the selection and jumps to the first match. Standard macOS pattern. Up to 120 chars prefilled.
  • Help menu now has real entries: Termy Help on GitHub, Report an Issue…, Release Notes — direct links to README, new-issue form, and the changelog.
  • README documents the right-click context menu and ⌘E + ⌘⇧L shortcuts.

item.title: Version 0.9.24
item.description:
Right-click in terminal → context menu

  • Native right-click menu in any pane: Copy, Paste, Select All, Find in Scrollback, Clear, New Tab, Split Horizontally, Split Vertically. Every native terminal has this — Termy was missing it through v0.9.23.
  • Uses SwiftTerm's public copy(_:) / paste(_:) / selectAll(_:) action methods so selection / pasteboard handling stays consistent with keyboard shortcut paths.

item.title: Version 0.9.23
item.description:
Small UX polish

  • Tab right-click menu gains Duplicate Tab and Copy cwd path.
  • Status-bar cwd right-click menu: Reveal in Finder, Copy path, Open in new tab.
  • Settings → About now links to "What's new in vX.Y.Z →" pointing at the matching GitHub release.

item.title: Version 0.9.22
item.description:
Cross-log content search + verified 100-tab stress

  • Search across all session logs. The Session Logs filter now greps file contents too, not just filenames. Type a query → every log with a match shows a per-file hit count badge. Results sort by match density. Selected log highlights every occurrence inline. Vibecoder use case: "find my old Claude conversation about WebSockets" — type "websocket", click the top result, scroll to the highlighted match.
  • Debounced 150ms so typing stays smooth, runs off-main-thread, skips logs > 5 MB (those use the head/tail preview path).
  • Verified at 100 tabs: Termy RSS ~148 MB with 100 live PTYs, tab bar scrolls smoothly with overflow chevrons, app stays responsive. ~1.5 MB per tab is acceptable.

item.title: Version 0.9.21
item.description:
Session Logs browser + tab overflow chevrons + dynamic tab titles

  • Session Logs browser. ⌘⇧L (also in Command Palette + Termy menu) opens a sidebar+detail browser of every past session you recorded. Sort by date, filter, preview (head + tail with ANSI stripped for readability), reveal in Finder or open in default app. Same 720×520 modal chrome as Settings. Empty-state walks you to enable recording.
  • Tab overflow chevrons. When you have more tabs than fit the bar, subtle gradient + chevron cues appear at each edge to indicate scrollable content. Active-tab auto-scroll-into-view kicks in when you select tabs offscreen.
  • Dynamic tab titles (docs). README now shows the one-liner zsh setup that makes tab titles update with the currently-running command (npm start, claude, etc.) instead of every tab being named "zsh".

item.title: Version 0.9.20
item.description:
Profile picker on + button, Reset all settings

  • Right-click the + new-tab button to pick a profile. Previously the only way to open a tab with a non-default profile was the Dock right-click menu — now it's one right-click in the tab bar.
  • Settings → About → Reset all Termy settings. Restores every preference (themes, profiles, tab layouts, notifications, etc.) to factory defaults. Quits Termy afterwards; relaunch starts fresh. Files on disk untouched.

item.title: Version 0.9.19
item.description:
Drag-resizable pane dividers

  • Drag the divider between split panes to change the size ratio — 70/30, 60/40, anything between 10% and 90%. Cursor turns into the resize arrow on hover, divider tints brighter while held. Replaces the previous fixed equal-split.
  • Fractions persist per-tab across launches — a 75/25 split comes back the way you left it.
  • New splits carve from the active pane's space (not equally rebalanced), so creating a fourth pane doesn't unexpectedly shrink the three you already laid out.
  • Closing a pane donates its space to the neighbour that inherits focus — adjacent panes don't suddenly jump in size.

item.title: Version 0.9.18
item.description:
Visible close affordances + grouped title-strip dividers

  • Split panes get a close ×. Hover any pane in a multi-pane tab → a small × appears in the top-right. Click to close just that pane. Previously the only path was "click pane to focus, then ⌘W" — non-obvious. Now reachable by sight.
  • Quake drop-down gets a close ×. Top-right of the panel, glass-backed circle. Beyond ⌃` and ⎋ users now have a visible exit affordance.
  • Title-strip icons grouped with dividers. Three logical groups (navigation / layout / help) separated by thin vertical dividers so the row reads as clusters instead of a wall of glyphs. Future-proof: new icons drop into the right cluster — users don't have to re-scan when something is added.

item.title: Version 0.9.17
item.description:
Modal consistency + UX reachability pass

Modal pass

  • Cheatsheet redesign. Was a single 520-wide scrolling column; now matches the Settings / Command Palette shell (640×480, sidebar + detail) so all three modals share the same chrome. Categories: Tabs, Splits, Search & nav, Display, Windows.

UX reachability

  • ⌘, opens Settings (standard Apple convention).
  • ⌘/ opens the Keyboard Cheatsheet — discoverability without taking a hand off the keyboard.
  • Status-bar mode indicators. When session recording / cinema mode / always-on-top is active, a tiny matching SF Symbol (red record dot / film / pin) lights up in the status bar so the user sees at a glance what non-default modes are on. Hover each for context + how to turn off.

Image rendering caveat (v0.9.16 follow-up)

  • v0.9.16 advertised inline images as working via SwiftTerm's native OSC 1337 / Sixel / Kitty support. Verification showed SwiftTerm parses OSC 1337 correctly (Terminal.swift:1819) and even decodes the image into an NSImage (AppleTerminalView.swift:2044), but the final visual insertion didn't render in our overlay setup. Likely an interaction with Termy's adaptive backdrop / SwiftUI host. Logged as known-issue; impersonation env stays (so tools opt in) but visible images are pending a SwiftTerm view-layer investigation.

item.title: Version 0.9.16
item.description:
Inline images via imgcat/viu/yazi + Cinema mode for screen recordings

  • Image rendering works. Termy now identifies as TERM_PROGRAM=iTerm.app (+ LC_TERMINAL=iTerm2) so tools like imgcat, chafa, viu, yazi, kitten icat emit OSC 1337 / Sixel / Kitty graphics protocols — and SwiftTerm renders them inline. The dedicated TERMY env var lets shell config detect actual-Termy without breaking the image ecosystem. Same approach WezTerm + Ghostty take.
  • Cinema mode. Settings → General → Cinema mode: pace incoming PTY output at a fixed characters-per-second rate (30-500 cps, default 80). Streaming text looks "typewriter-smooth" on camera instead of frame-coalesced bursts. Tracking (notifications, OSC 133, session recording) still uses real arrival time — only the visible display is paced. Off by default. Bypassed for large bursts (paste, file dumps) so it never hurts normal output.
  • Stress-tested: 30 tabs open simultaneously, tab bar scrolls horizontally, active pane stays responsive, no crashes.

item.title: Version 0.9.15
item.description:
Drag-drop tab reorder, session recording, accessibility, image-tool compat

  • Drag tabs to reorder. Pick up any tab chip and drop it anywhere in the row. Order persists across launches.
  • Optional session recording. Settings → General → Session recording. Each pane writes its raw output to ~/Library/Application Support/Termy/sessions/ while enabled. Reveal-folder button in the same pane.
  • VoiceOver labels. Every chrome icon button and tab chip now exposes a friendly screen-reader label (Search, Settings, Active tab: foo, etc.) instead of raw SF Symbol names.
  • Image-tool compatibility. Termy now sets TERM_PROGRAM + TERM_FEATURES env vars so tools like imgcat, chafa, yazi, viu can detect they're running in a host with OSC 1337 / Sixel / Kitty image-protocol support (SwiftTerm renders all three).

item.title: Version 0.9.14
item.description:
Custom tab titles + always-on-top window

  • Rename any tab. Double-click a tab chip (or right-click → Rename Tab…) to give it a stable name like "claude code", "metrics", "build". Persists across launches. Right-click → Reset to auto title to revert.
  • Always-on-top window. Window menu → Toggle Always on Top — keeps the active Termy window pinned above all other apps. Toggle off to return to normal z-order. Useful for keeping a reference shell visible while you work elsewhere.

item.title: Version 0.9.13
item.description:
OSC 133 shell integration + orphan-key GC

  • OSC 133 / FinalTerm shell integration. Termy now parses ESC ] 133 ; X sequences and fires accurate command-finished notifications the instant your shell reports done — no idle-heuristic guessing. Add three lines to .zshrc (see README) and `claude`, `codex`, `npm test` etc. ping the moment they return, with the last output line as the notification body. Heuristic stays as fallback for shells without setup.
  • Multi-window orphan-key garbage collection. Saved windowKeys list is now capped + pruned at launch — abandoned UserDefaults entries from closed-but-not-unregistered windows no longer leak forever.
  • Dead code stripped: unused `heightObserver` declaration in QuickTerminalController.

item.title: Version 0.9.12 (hotfix)
item.description:
Crash fix — ProfileAvatar arithmetic overflow

Crash log analysis surfaced a Swift runtime trap in ProfileAvatar.angleOffset: the FNV-1a seed hash could be close to Int.max, and the * 17 spread multiply was overflowing Int and trapping. Affected v0.9.7–v0.9.11 intermittently on theme switches and settings sheet renders. Fixed by reducing modulo 360 before the multiply and using overflow-wrapping arithmetic.

item.title: Version 0.9.11
item.description:
Smart notifications, simpler Settings, tab right-click menu, ⌘1-⌘9, reveal in Finder

Smart notifications

  • Auto "command finished" notifications. Heuristic that fires when a pane goes from actively producing output to quiet for N seconds — works for Claude Code, Codex, npm test, cargo build, anything. No shell setup, no OSC 133.
  • Notification body shows the last terminal line so you see what finished, not just that it did.
  • Configurable quiet threshold (2-30s), sound on/off, focus scope, preview on/off.

UX upgrades

  • ⌘1-⌘9 to jump to tab N (⌘9 = last tab, browser convention).
  • Tab right-click menu: Reveal cwd in Finder, Close Other Tabs, Tab Color, Broadcast Input.
  • Status bar cwd is clickable — opens the directory in Finder.

Settings simplified

  • 11 panes → 5: General (system + chrome + notifications + updates + vibecoder), Appearance (theme + font + density + background), Profiles, Quake, About. Less wandering, more finding.

Marketing-y README

  • Rewrote the GitHub front page with hero pitch, badges, comparison ("iTerm2 alternative", "Warp alternative"), and SEO keywords so people can actually find Termy when searching for a modern macOS terminal.

item.title: Version 0.9.10
item.description:
Tight title-strip — Claude + Codex only, unified icon style

  • AI launcher row trimmed to Claude Code + OpenAI Codex. Cursor, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot, and Aider are removed — Cursor is a GUI launcher and the others were never the actual workflow.
  • Launcher icons now use the same flat rounded-square chrome treatment as the feature buttons (search, palette, splits, etc.) — the whole title strip reads as one consistent control row, with a thin divider between AI launchers and feature actions.

item.title: Version 0.9.9
item.description:
Multi-window restore that actually works + Quake settings

  • Multi-window state survives launches. Each window now owns its own UserDefaults restore key (UUID-scoped). Through v0.9.8 every window wrote to the same key and the last save won, so multi-window users lost state on relaunch. Now every window comes back, its tabs/panes intact. Legacy single-key data is migrated on first launch.
  • Per-pane profile persistence. Splits remember which profile each pane was opened with, not just the cwd — restoring a split now reapplies the right shell/env/tag color.
  • Per-tab broadcast-input and tag color persistence — survive restarts.
  • Quake settings pane. New Settings → Quake section: toggle hide-on-focus-loss (keep the panel pinned while you reference it from elsewhere), and a height slider 20%–95% of the active screen.

item.title: Version 0.9.8
item.description:
Polish: live git branch, multi-monitor Quake, shortcuts cheatsheet

  • Status bar git branch refreshes live — `git checkout` / `git switch` now updates the displayed branch within ~4s, without needing to `cd` to bump the cwd. Cheap on-disk poll, off the main thread.
  • Quake drop-down opens on the active monitor — previously always opened on the primary display, ignoring where the cursor was. Now lands on whichever screen contains the mouse pointer.
  • Keyboard cheatsheet — new ? icon in the title strip surfaces a modal listing every shortcut grouped by category. Discoverability for users who don't memorise key combos.

item.title: Version 0.9.7
item.description:
Real Quake drop-down, inline find bar, visible icon affordances

  • Quake-style drop-down terminal (⌃`) — a single persistent panel that slides down from the top of the active display, with its own shell that stays alive between toggles. Replaces the v0.9.6 "Quick Terminal" that secretly just opened a regular second window.
  • Inline find bar (⌘F) — search-as-you-type across the scrollback, with case + regex toggles and ⌘G / ⌘⇧G to cycle matches. Wired to SwiftTerm's real find API. Replaces the legacy NSFindPanel popup.
  • Visible icon row in the title strip — search, command palette, recent directories, split horizontal/vertical, Quake drop-down, settings. Every keyboard shortcut also gets a clickable affordance so features are discoverable by scanning the chrome.
  • SSH config Include directives are now followed — palette SSH list reflects modern ~/.ssh/config.d/* style multi-account setups (recursive, depth-limited, dedup'd).
  • Bug fix: accidental ⌘W on a last-tab window no longer permanently wipes the saved layout — the rescue blank tab is no longer persisted over the previous state.
  • Bug fix: broadcast-input key monitor was installed before the host window existed; comparison silently no-op'd. Now reinstalled when the window arrives.
  • Cuts: the Workflow store (5 seeded snippets nobody asked for), the tapback.co avatar HTTP fetch (privacy + offline-launch issue, replaced with a local gradient generator), and ~100 lines of dead CursorPreview markers.

item.title: Version 0.9.6
item.description:
Quieter scrollback, no more silent app-quit on ⌘W, real font picker

  • Caret visible from launch — no more "click a theme to make the cursor appear." Appearance now re-applies on the first non-zero layout pass, so SwiftTerm gets a real frame to position the caret against.
  • Scrollback survives layout passes — v0.9.3's reflow-off wrapper fired on every SwiftUI sub-cell layout (hover state, pixel snaps), trimming scrollback to the viewport hundreds of times a second. Now gated on actual cell-grid changes, so scrollback is only trimmed when columns/rows would genuinely change.
  • ⌘W on a single-tab window no longer quits Termy. Used to cascade: close pane → close tab → close window → terminate app. Now the last tab is replaced with a fresh blank tab; ⌘⇧W still closes the window outright.
  • Saved tab layout survives an unmounted external drive. Previously, launching Termy with a saved cwd on an unmounted volume would silently overwrite the saved layout with a blank tab. Now we open a blank tab without persisting, so the layout comes back next time the volume is mounted.
  • Font picker shows every installed monospaced font — Nerd Fonts, Berkeley Mono, JetBrains Mono, etc. — instead of a hardcoded 7-entry list.
  • Drag-drop quoting hardened. Dropped file paths are now always single-quoted, so a path containing $(...) or backticks can't run shell substitution before you read the command.
  • Removed two dead settings (lineSpacing, copyOnSelect) that were persisted but never wired through anywhere — kept around as ghosts since v0.8.x.

item.title: Version 0.9.4
item.description:
Cursor on launch, bulletproof resize, stronger adaptive backdrop

Heads up: this is the first Termy build with Sparkle EdDSA signing turned on. If you're on 0.9.3 or earlier and Sparkle won't apply this update, grab the DMG from the GitHub release page once — every update after this one will install automatically.

  • Caret renders from the first frame now, not only after toggling themes. Theme + caret colors are re-applied once the view enters its window, so the cursor is visible the moment Termy opens.
  • Scrollback no longer corrupts when you resize a window and scroll back up. Reflow is now skipped entirely around every resize (SwiftTerm's reflowWider is the buggy path). Visible viewport content is preserved; history past it is dropped on resize — strictly better than the ghosted/duplicated rows you'd get before.
  • Whole-window adaptive darkening — not a per-element box. The wallpaper-brightness opacity range widened (0.10–0.35 → 0.18–0.78) with a curve that stays glassy through the dark half and ramps hard only on genuinely light wallpapers. Brightness re-samples when you switch apps, displays, or Spaces.

item.title: Version 0.9.2
item.description:
Default shell-script handler + cursor + resize fixes

  • Termy now registers as the system handler for .sh / .command / +x binaries — double-click one in Finder and it opens a new tab in the file's parent dir and runs the script as the first command.
  • Caret is no longer invisible on dark themes. Pinned caretColor to the theme's foreground (and ansi[0] as the inverse text color) so the cursor is legible on every theme/wallpaper combination.
  • Scrollback survives window resize. SwiftTerm's reflowWider was being called hundreds of times per second during a corner drag, corrupting wrapped lines. Coalesced into a single reflow on resize end.

item.title: Version 0.9.1
item.description:
Readable on any backdrop

  • Terminal text is now legible whether you're over a dark wallpaper, a white app, or anything else — switched the window backdrop from .ultraThinMaterial to NSVisualEffectView's .hudWindow material, which stays dark-glassy regardless of what's behind.
  • README rewritten with full feature list, shortcuts, settings tour, and honest known-limitations section.

item.title: Version 0.9.0
item.description:
Dock right-click menu + bell notifications

  • Right-click Termy in the Dock: New Window, New Tab, Quick Terminal, plus a "New Tab with Profile" submenu when you've saved more than one profile.
  • "Notify when an unfocused window beeps" toggle (General settings) — pair with a zsh precmd that rings the bell and you get system notifications when long commands finish in background windows.

item.title: Version 0.8.6
item.description:
Profile deletion safety

  • Deleting a profile now asks "are you sure?" — no more accidental nukes from misclicking the trash icon.
  • Default profile delete shows a contextual message about the demotion.
  • Store refuses to remove the last profile (would have desynced ID-based comparisons).

item.title: Version 0.8.5
item.description:
Bigger profile-row hit targets

  • The pencil + trash icons on each profile row are now 22pt tap targets instead of ~10pt — actually clickable without zooming in.

item.title: Version 0.8.4
item.description:
Escape closes modals reliably

  • Command Palette and Recent Directories now close on Escape regardless of focus — previously the TextField inside the palette was eating the keystroke.

item.title: Version 0.8.3
item.description:
Settings honesty pass

  • Removed the Cursor settings pane — SwiftTerm doesn't expose a public hook to apply cursor style/blink, so the toggles were doing nothing. Better gone than misleading.
  • Audited every other settings pane — General, Profiles, Vibecoder, Theme, Font, Density, Chrome, Background, Updates each have real downstream consumers.

item.title: Version 0.8.2 (hotfix)
item.description:
Critical fix on top of 0.8.0

  • 0.8.0/0.8.1 broke keyboard input — overriding terminalDelegate disconnected keystrokes from the PTY. Reverted.
  • All other 0.8.0 improvements (profiles applied to new tabs, multi-window close fix, pane minimums, debounced persist, worktree-safe git, etc.) intact.

item.title: Version 0.8.0
item.description:
Stability + correctness

  • Profiles now actually apply to new tabs (shell, args, env, cwd, tag color) — were previously decorative
  • Cursor style + blink toggles work via DECSCUSR escape sequences
  • Multi-window close fixed: closes the right window, not whichever one is keyWindow
  • Pane minimums (200×100pt) so splits stay usable at minimum window width
  • Hyperlink clicks: terminalDelegate ours now, scheme-filtered to http/https/file
  • Status bar git detection handles worktrees + submodules (no more "gitdir:" branch name)
  • Performance: appearance + cwd persist debounced

item.title: Version 0.7.2
item.description:
Quick Terminal + polish

  • New Termy → Quick Terminal (⌃\`) — opens a fresh floating window without disturbing your main session
  • Profile editor remembers the right state when reopened (no more stale drafts)
  • Narrow windows no longer truncate the launcher row — session title gives up width first

item.title: Version 0.7.1
item.description:
Layout fixes + memoji profile avatars

  • AI launcher row now sits on the same row as the macOS traffic lights (no more separate band below)
  • Hover labels show the brand name as a non-glitchy inline pill next to the icons
  • Real LobeHub brand SVGs for Claude Code, OpenAI/Codex, Cursor, Gemini CLI, GitHub Copilot
  • Profiles UI in Settings with random Tapback memoji avatars per profile (re-roll button in the editor)
  • Auto-update toggles: auto-check + auto-install in background — never reinstall again
  • Command Palette now matches the Settings sheet exactly (640×480, sidebar+detail)

item.title: Version 0.6.0
item.description:
Density previews + resize smoothness

  • Density preview now shows a real mini-terminal at each preset's actual padding — compact/cozy/spacious are visibly distinct
  • Resize is smooth: AppKit autoresizing drives SwiftTerm directly, no stale text on window drag
  • AI quick launch actually executes the command (sends CR, not LF)
  • Removed redundant cwd label from title strip

item.title: Version 0.5.0
item.description:
Visual previews + auto-updater

  • Theme/cursor/density/font/opacity visual previews in Settings
  • Icon-only AI launcher row in white
  • Sparkle auto-updater wired up
  • Profiles model (UI coming next release)
